facts.statement: Union Pacific Corporation is classified under SIC code 'Railroads, Line-Haul Operating' and is listed on the NYSE under the ticker symbol UNP.
Union Pacific Corporation is incorporated in Utah, with principal executive offices at 1400 Douglas Street, Omaha, Nebraska 68179.
Union Pacific reported total revenue of $24.119 billion for FY2025.
Union Pacific reported net income of $6.379 billion for FY2025.
Union Pacific generated $8.379 billion in operating cash flow for FY2025.
As of FY2025 year-end, Union Pacific had stockholders' equity of $12.163 billion, long-term debt of $31.814 billion, and cash and equivalents of $1.055 billion.
In 2025, Union Pacific achieved an operating ratio of 59.8%, a 10-basis point improvement versus 2024, and reported an 8% increase in earnings per share versus 2024.

Total volume increased 1% in 2025 versus 2024, driven by coal, industrial chemicals and plastics, grain and grain products, and rock shipments, partially offset by weaker demand for automotive and energy and specialized markets shipments.
Union Pacific reports revenue across three principal business segments: Bulk, Industrial, and Premium, as well as Other subsidiary revenues and accessorial revenues.

In 2025, Union Pacific achieved best-ever personal injury and derailment incident rate results, with a personal injury rate of 0.68 (improved 24% vs. 2024) and a derailment incident rate of 1.75 (improved 19% vs. 2024).
In 2025, freight car velocity improved 8% and terminal dwell declined 8% compared to 2024, while Service Performance Index improved 9 points for intermodal and 11 points for manifest products.

Union Pacific holds an equity-method investment in TTX Company, a rail car pooling company, valued at $2.0 billion as of December 31, 2025 (up from $1.9 billion in 2024), with TTX car hire expense of $447 million in 2025.

On July 28, 2025, Union Pacific entered into a merger agreement to acquire Norfolk Southern Corporation, with each Norfolk Southern share to be converted into one share of UNP common stock plus $88.82 in cash; the transaction is expected to issue approximately 224.8 million UNP shares and pay approximately $20 billion in cash.

The STB issued a decision on January 16, 2026 that the joint application for the Norfolk Southern merger was not accepted as complete; the applicants are permitted to file a revised application, and the acquisition is currently expected to be completed in 2027.
Both Union Pacific and Norfolk Southern may be required to pay the other a termination fee of $2.5 billion if the merger agreement is terminated under certain circumstances.

Union Pacific incurred $72 million in total acquisition-related expense associated with the Norfolk Southern merger agreement for the year ended December 31, 2025, including $68 million in purchased services and materials and $4 million in other costs.
V. James Vena serves as Chief Executive Officer of Union Pacific Corporation, and Jennifer L. Hamann serves as Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer.
Carrie J. Powers serves as Vice President, Controller, and Chief Accounting Officer of Union Pacific Corporation.

Eric G. Gehringer serves as Executive Vice President - Operations at Union Pacific, and on December 19, 2025 adopted a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan to sell up to 1,999 shares of UNP common stock between March 20, 2026 and December 31, 2026.

Union Pacific's Board of Directors includes eleven directors: David B. Dillon, Doyle R. Simons, Sheri H. Edison, John K. Tien Jr., Teresa M. Finley, John P. Wiehoff, Deborah C. Hopkins, W. Anthony Will, Jane H. Lute, Christopher J. Williams, and Michael R. McCarthy.
Union Pacific conducted an accelerated share repurchase program in 2025, with transactions initiated on February 18, 2025 and additional activity in Q1 and Q2 2025.

As of January 30, 2026, Union Pacific had 593,391,460 shares of common stock outstanding, and as of June 30, 2025, the aggregate market value of common stock held by non-affiliates was $136.1 billion.

As of December 31, 2025, Union Pacific had 2,487,690 securities to be issued upon exercise of outstanding options, warrants and rights under equity compensation plans, with a weighted-average exercise price of $210.32, and 29,874,539 securities remaining available for future issuance.

Union Pacific's 2026 Annual Meeting of Shareholders is scheduled for May 14, 2026, with a record date of March 13, 2026, at which eleven directors are to be elected, Deloitte & Touche LLP is to be ratified as auditor, and an advisory vote on executive compensation is to be held.

Union Pacific reported adjusted (non-GAAP) FY2025 results excluding acquisition-related expense, industrial park land sales, deferred tax adjustments, and a crew staffing agreement, resulting in adjusted net income of $6.947 billion, adjusted diluted EPS of $11.66, and an adjusted operating ratio of 59.3%.

Deloitte & Touche LLP issued an unqualified opinion on Union Pacific's internal control over financial reporting as of December 31, 2025, and management assessed internal controls as effective based on the COSO 2013 framework.
